meta.title: BMW N73/N74 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: BMW N73/N74 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The BMW N73/N74 is a smooth, powerful V12 – but complexity and high running costs are the defining trade‑offs. Backed by UK specialist data [ESTIMATED].

rows.value: 7 Series, 8 Series, X7, Rolls‑Royce Ghost, Phantom, Cullinan [BMW‑VERIFIED]
Also fitted to Rolls‑Royce (Ghost, Phantom, Cullinan, Dawn, Wraith)

verdictRating.confidence: 85% – based on specialist reports and UK owner surveys.

rows.score: 8 (V12 complexity makes most repairs labour-intensive; limited specialist pool)
8 (Parts are available but often dealer-only or import-dependent; aftermarket support is limited)
8 (Recon cost ~60–74% of lowest typical value (older E65 760i) → 8)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The N73/N74 V12 is a magnificent engine — but running costs are eye-watering and repairs are specialist-only — buy only with deep pockets and a warranty.
verdictRating.bestFor: Luxury car buyers who value effortless torque and refinement above all else.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You're on a budget, or don't have access to a specialist who knows V12 BMWs.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Also powers the Rolls‑Royce Ghost, Phantom, Cullinan, Dawn, and Wraith – the N74 is the foundation of Rolls‑Royce's modern V12 line‑up.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an N73/N74 typically takes 18–24 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: The N74 is used in both BMW and Rolls‑Royce applications – parts are identical, though Rolls‑Royce-specific tuning and ancillaries may add cost.

items.question: How much does an N73/N74 engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned N73/N74 typically costs £7,000–£10,000 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by exact variant and specification. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500 depending on labour rates and engine bay access.

items.question: Is the BMW N73/N74 a reliable engine?
items.answer: We rate the N73/N74 at 3.5 stars out of 5, based on an 85% confidence level from specialist reports and owner surveys [ESTIMATED]. The core engine is robust, but the complexity of the V12 means many potential failure points – and parts are expensive.

items.question: What is the most common N73/N74 failure?
items.answer: Valvetronic motor and eccentric shaft wear (N73), coil pack failures, and cooling system leaks (water pumps, expansion tanks) are the most common. The N74's twin‑turbo system also suffers from wastegate rattle and oil feed line blockages.

items.question: Which BMW models use the N73/N74?
items.answer: The N73 powered the E65/E66 760i; the N74 powered the F01/F02 760i, G11/G12 M760Li, G15 M850i, and G07 X7 M60i. It is also the engine behind every modern Rolls‑Royce V12.

items.question: How long does the N73/N74 last?
items.answer: With meticulous maintenance (regular oil changes, cooling system monitoring, and addressing issues promptly), the N73/N74 can exceed 150,000 miles. However, repair costs are high – preventative maintenance is far cheaper than waiting for failure.
